Before writing, reason through the fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edit, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists the change you made, or note if the fix was already applied or could not be applied (with a brief reason).
Let me start by reading the file to understand its full context.

Now let me reason through the fix:

**Migration rule analysis:**
- Rule `sd-cf-descriptionlist-descriptionlist-req-group` says that `DescriptionList` must contain `DescriptionListGroup` children.
- In PF v6, `DescriptionList` requires its children to be wrapped in `DescriptionListGroup` components.

**Current code:**
- The `DescriptionList` (line 28) directly contains multiple `DetailsItem` components (lines 29-73).
- `DetailsItem` is a custom component from `copiedFromConsole/utils/details-item`, not a PatternFly component.

**What to do:**
- The `DetailsItem` components are custom wrappers, not PF `DescriptionListTerm`/`DescriptionListDescription` components. The conformance rule says `DescriptionList` must contain `DescriptionListGroup`.
- The simplest correct fix is to wrap all the children of `DescriptionList` in a single `DescriptionListGroup`. Each `DetailsItem` likely renders its own term/description pair internally, so wrapping all items in one group preserves the existing behavior.
- Add the `DescriptionListGroup` import.

Let me apply the fix:

arguments.after:
<DescriptionList aria-label={t("NodeHealthCheck details")}>
      <DescriptionListGroup>
        <DetailsItem label={t("Name")} obj={resource} path={"metadata.name"} />
        <DetailsItem
          label={t("Labels")}
          obj={resource}
          path="metadata.labels"
          valueClassName="details-item__value--labels"
          canEdit={canEdit}
          editAsGroup
          onEdit={() => modalsContext.openModal(ModalId.EDIT_LABELS, resource)}
        >
          <LabelList kind={nodeHealthCheckStringKind} labels={metadata.labels} />
        </DetailsItem>
        <DetailsItem
          label={t("Annotations")}
          obj={resource}
          path="metadata.annotations"
          onEdit={() =>
            modalsContext.openModal(ModalId.EDIT_ANNOTATIONS, resource)
          }
        >
          {t("{{count}} annotation", {
            count: size(metadata.annotations),
          })}
        </DetailsItem>
        <DetailsItem
          label={t("Node selector")}
          obj={resource}
          path={"spec.selector"}
        >
          <Selector kind={t("Node")} selector={get(resource, "spec.selector")} />
        </DetailsItem>
        <DetailsItem
          label={t("Created at")}
          obj={resource}
          path="metadata.creationTimestamp"
        >
          <Timestamp timestamp={metadata.creationTimestamp} />
        </DetailsItem>
        <DetailsItem
          label={t("Owner")}
          obj={resource}
          path="metadata.ownerReferences"
        >
          <OwnerReferences obj={resource} />
        </DetailsItem>
      </DescriptionListGroup>
    </DescriptionList>

      >

      </DetailsItem>

        obj={resource}
        path="metadata.annotations"

          modalsContext.openModal(ModalId.EDIT_ANNOTATIONS, resource)
        }
      >
        {t("{{count}} annotation", {
          count: size(metadata.annotations),
        })}
      </DetailsItem>
      <DetailsItem
        label={t("Node selector")}
        obj={resource}
        path={"spec.selector"}
      >
        <Selector kind={t("Node")} selector={get(resource, "spec.selector")} />
      </DetailsItem>
      <DetailsItem
        label={t("Created at")}
        obj={resource}
        path="metadata.creationTimestamp"
      >

      <DetailsItem
        label={t("Owner")}

        <OwnerReferences obj={resource} />
